pairs.question: Who is Joseph Hebert and what notable achievement did he accomplish in poker in 2020?
pairs.answer: Joseph Hebert is an American professional poker player from Metairie, Louisiana. In 2020, he finished runner-up in the World Series of Poker (WSOP) Main Event, earning $1,553,256 for his second-place finish.

pairs.question: What was Joseph Hebert's first WSOP cash, and how many career WSOP cashes did he have before the 2020 Main Event?
pairs.answer: Hebert's first WSOP cash came in 2014. Before the 2020 Main Event, he had 18 career WSOP cashes, including three from the 2020 WSOP Online.

pairs.question: How did Joseph Hebert qualify for the WSOP.com portion of the 2020 Main Event?
pairs.answer: He qualified for the WSOP.com portion of the 2020 Main Event by winning a $300 satellite tournament.

pairs.question: Describe the final hand of the WSOP.com 2020 Main Event where Joseph Hebert eliminated Ron Jenkins.
pairs.answer: On the first hand of heads-up play, Hebert knocked out Ron Jenkins with A♣ Q♠ against Q♦ Q♣. An ace came on the flop, giving Hebert a pair of aces to win the hand.

pairs.question: What was the outcome of Joseph Hebert's heads-up championship match against Damian Salas in January 2021?
pairs.answer: Hebert lost the heads-up championship match to Damian Salas after his A♦ Q♠ was beaten by K♦ J♠ on the 173rd hand of play.

pairs.question: Besides poker, what other job has Joseph Hebert held, and where?
pairs.answer: Hebert has worked part-time as a waiter at The Galley Seafood restaurant in Metairie, Louisiana.

pairs.question: What personal event did Joseph Hebert dedicate his 2020 WSOP Main Event run to?
pairs.answer: Hebert dedicated his 2020 WSOP Main Event run to the memory of his mother, Linda, who passed away in July 2020.
